column4: Supplier minimum-purity statement retained by NACA TN 2680
column5: The supplier claimed a minimum purity of 99.6 mole percent. The report does not identify the supplier, lot, analytical method, measured assay, impurity composition, storage history, or uncertainty, so the lower-bound statement is not promoted to an exact composition

column4: Schlieren photographs of atmospheric-pressure Bunsen-type flames reduced by the total-area method; experimental maximum-flame-speed rows transcribed from NACA TN 2680 Table I
column5: The report states that each maximum occurred at an equivalence ratio between 1.0 and 1.1 but does not identify the exact maximizing ratio per row. The atmospheric pressure was an experiment-wide average of 744 mm mercury, transcribed here as 99.2 kPa at source-supported precision, not a per-row reading. The 0.210 oxygen-fraction runs used laboratory service air containing approximately 0.4 volume percent water; because the value is approximate it remains observation-only. Other oxygen-nitrogen mixtures were supplier analyzed to plus or minus 0.1 volume percent. Repeated rows at the same nominal condition remain separate observations-in-table claims. Raw photographs, row-level pressure, dispersion, confidence metadata, supplier, lot, exact assay, and impurity composition are absent. These are mixture-and-apparatus observations, not geometry-free bulk-material constants or a continuous flame-speed law
